title: Dense Filipino Peanuts Peanut Stew Kare-Kare
description: A rich, smoky, and dense Filipino stew featuring almond milk, perfect for a weekend brunch. The peanut and almond flavors blend harmoniously to create a delightful meal.
instructions: In a blender or food processor, combine peanuts, peanut butter, and a pinch of salt. Blend until smooth and creamy.
Heat the infuse oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and saut0e until fragrant, about 1 minute.
Pour in the peanut mixture and cook for about 2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Slowly add the almond milk, whisking continuously to prevent lumps. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and let the stew simmer for about 10 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the tapioca starch and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the stew has reached the desired consistency.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot over rice or noodles, garnished with additional peanuts and a drizzle of infuse oil, if desired.
Tapioca starch can be replaced with cornstarch or flour if needed.
The stew can be made ahead of time and refrigerated or frozen for later use.
When reheating, stir in a little extra water if the stew has thickened too much.
This recipe makes 4-6 servings, so feel free to adjust the ingredients as needed.
Be sure to use high-quality peanut butter and peanuts for the best flavor.
Enjoy your delicious and satisfying Filipino peanut stew!
